title: Luke Subconscious and reference integration roadmap

policy.clean_codebase: no tmp files, no dead code, no dead files, no unnecessary folders, subfolders, or files
core_rules: Subconscious signals are review-only and cannot become Coder jobs without Main approval.
A thought is not a task and a build marker is not approval.
Reference systems are idea sources only unless explicitly wired in with approval.
No live trading behavior is allowed during research, parser, UI, memory, Radar, context, or Subconscious work.
Auto-build cannot be implemented until signal board, Main approval, Coder job state, QA packet, runtime monitor, sprint lock, rollback path, and tests exist.
policy.protected_note: Subconscious signals are review-only and cannot become Coder jobs without Main approval. Auto-build cannot be implemented until signal board, Main approval, Coder job state, QA packet, runtime monitor, sprint lock, rollback path, and tests exist.
known_loopholes: recordRadarIngest currently writes runtime state immediately via appendJsonl and snapshot rebuilds.
recordRadarReview currently writes runtime state and snapshots.
Brain /radar/ingest and /radar/review are live write routes and need dry-run/write gates before Subconscious use.
Brain /agent/brain/report is evidence only and must never count as Main approval.
Radar SOURCE_TYPES does not include subconscious_signal; use reference_idea review-only until a dedicated type is approved.
Package scripts include write-capable and sensitive surfaces; inspect scripts before running PM2, Ninja, Pine, broker, credential, market-data, or install commands.

already_landed.evidence: Root AGENTS.md contains Process_narration=false, Measure twice/cut once, clean-codebase rule, dirty-worktree protection, reference systems as idea sources only, and trading no-touch boundaries.
lib/reference-repo-registry.js lists ten reference repos with allowed_surfaces and banned_surfaces; entries ban trading_execution and broker.
lib/radar/ingest.js supports reference_idea, review_only, review states, scope/status/recall_reason/relationship_ids, and trading_authority none.
lib/paths.js centralizes state/events/*.jsonl and state/snapshots/*.json paths; production writes remain forbidden until approved.
agents/agent-00-brain.js exposes POST /agent/brain/report for subagent reports; reports are evidence only, not approval.
tests/autonomous-recommendation-only.test.js checks recommendation_only/staged_only posture and blocked execution routes.
docs/HERMES_SUBCONSCIOUS_SOURCE_ROADMAP.md, docs/LUKE_SUBCONSCIOUS_AUTOBUILD_ROADMAP.md, and docs/LUKE_SUBCONSCIOUS_PROPOSAL_SCHEMA.md define safe separation and dry-run/write-gate requirements.

goals.title: Baseline and safety freeze
goals.objective: Establish exact repo truth before Subconscious/Hermes/Luke auto-build work continues.
goals.rationale: Advance the Luke Subconscious/auto-build roadmap without weakening trading safety or adding hidden runtime behavior.
goals.stop_condition: Baseline evidence is recorded with git status, reference repo list/commits, Radar routes, UI surfaces, Katbot seams, and safe integration seams.
goals.rollback_expectation: Revert only this phase's owned plan/doc/code changes; do not touch unrelated work. If runtime state is written unexpectedly, stop and report before further action. If protected paths appear in git diff --name-only, stop before commit/push unless the user explicitly approved that exact surface.
non_goals: No trading execution, broker routing, risk, credential, secret, or .env changes.
No production runtime state writes unless this phase explicitly allows them.
goals.result_summary: Baseline evidence recorded in reports/luke-ai-repo-integration-plan-2026-05-08.md. Repo was clean on main...origin/main; all ten reference repos were present with commits; Radar routes, UI surfaces, Katbot seams, and safe integration seams were recorded.
risks: Git emits a user-level C:/Users/conor/.config/git/ignore permission warning outside the repo.
assumptions: Current uncommitted changes are intentional planning/doc changes owned by this roadmap work.

goals.title: Reader and operator roadmaps
goals.objective: Keep the Hermes source roadmap and Luke adaptation roadmap available to fresh agents.
goals.rationale: Advance the Luke Subconscious/auto-build roadmap without weakening trading safety or adding hidden runtime behavior.
goals.stop_condition: Both roadmap docs are present and state thought/signal/approval/coding/QA/memory separation.
goals.rollback_expectation: Revert only this phase's owned plan/doc/code changes; do not touch unrelated work. If runtime state is written unexpectedly, stop and report before further action. If protected paths appear in git diff --name-only, stop before commit/push unless the user explicitly approved that exact surface.
evidence_required: Luke roadmap contains no-touch boundaries and phase map.
Hermes roadmap states a thought is not a task and build marker is not approval.
non_goals: No trading execution, broker routing, risk, credential, secret, or .env changes.
No production runtime state writes unless this phase explicitly allows them.
goals.result_summary: Both roadmap docs are present and define the safe translation: free-thinking, review-only signaling, Main approval, Coder from plan, QA proof.

goals.rationale: Advance the Luke Subconscious/auto-build roadmap without weakening trading safety or adding hidden runtime behavior.
goals.stop_condition: Plan records already-landed pieces, future phase contracts, no-touch paths, tests, stop conditions, rollback expectations, and evidence requirements.
goals.rollback_expectation: Revert only this phase's owned plan/doc/code changes; do not touch unrelated work. If runtime state is written unexpectedly, stop and report before further action. If protected paths appear in git diff --name-only, stop before commit/push unless the user explicitly approved that exact surface.

goals.title: Review-only Subconscious signal schema
goals.objective: Define and, only after approval, test a review-only Subconscious proposal schema that maps to existing Radar fields without production state writes.
goals.rationale: Advance the Luke Subconscious/auto-build roadmap without weakening trading safety or adding hidden runtime behavior.
allowed_paths: tests/ only if implementing schema validation in a later approved code chunk
required_tests: node --check lib/radar/ingest.js if code is touched later
cmd /c npx vitest run tests/*radar*.test.js only if Radar code/tests are touched later
A no-production-state-write check using temporary paths before any runtime write support
goals.stop_condition: A proposal schema exists, maps to `reference_idea`, preserves review-only status, and proves it cannot create Coder jobs or write production runtime state.
goals.rollback_expectation: Revert only this phase's owned plan/doc/code changes; do not touch unrelated work. If runtime state is written unexpectedly, stop and report before further action. If protected paths appear in git diff --name-only, stop before commit/push unless the user explicitly approved that exact surface.
evidence_required: Proof no state/events or state/snapshots write occurs.
non_goals: No trading execution, broker routing, risk, credential, secret, or .env changes.
No production runtime state writes unless this phase explicitly allows them.
risks: Current Radar drops arbitrary extra fields; proposal metadata must be encoded or Radar schema must be extended later.
assumptions: Use `source_type: reference_idea` until a dedicated source type is explicitly approved.

goals.title: Signal board snapshot
goals.objective: Design and later implement a deterministic signal board that distinguishes interesting, ready_for_main_review, blocked, cooldown, queued_after_approval, active, built, failed_qa, repaired, and archived.
goals.rationale: Advance the Luke Subconscious/auto-build roadmap without weakening trading safety or adding hidden runtime behavior.
allowed_paths: lib/radar/ or new non-trading module only after explicit approval
tests/ for temporary-path validation only after approval
required_tests: focused vitest for signal board using temporary paths
goals.stop_condition: Signal board exists or is fully specified, reads only approved inputs, and cannot mark anything ready_to_build without Main approval.
goals.rollback_expectation: Revert only this phase's owned plan/doc/code changes; do not touch unrelated work. If runtime state is written unexpectedly, stop and report before further action. If protected paths appear in git diff --name-only, stop before commit/push unless the user explicitly approved that exact surface.
non_goals: No trading execution, broker routing, risk, credential, secret, or .env changes.
No production runtime state writes unless this phase explicitly allows them.
risks: A board can become a task queue if states are vague. Use ready_for_main_review, not ready_to_build.
assumptions: Runtime state paths require explicit approval and dry-run first.

goals.title: Main approval and Coder job boundary
goals.objective: Define the approval artifact and job-state boundary that prevents direct Subconscious-to-code execution.
goals.rationale: Advance the Luke Subconscious/auto-build roadmap without weakening trading safety or adding hidden runtime behavior.
allowed_paths: tests/ only after approved code work
required_tests: focused tests for any job-state module later
goals.stop_condition: Main approval is a separate artifact with owner, scope, allowed paths, forbidden paths, tests, rollback, expiration, and explicit promotion statement.
goals.rollback_expectation: Revert only this phase's owned plan/doc/code changes; do not touch unrelated work. If runtime state is written unexpectedly, stop and report before further action. If protected paths appear in git diff --name-only, stop before commit/push unless the user explicitly approved that exact surface.
non_goals: No trading execution, broker routing, risk, credential, secret, or .env changes.
No production runtime state writes unless this phase explicitly allows them.
risks: Subagent reports can launder approval unless reports remain evidence only.
assumptions: No Coder job may be created without Main approval.

goals.title: QA and recovery loop
goals.objective: Define and later implement QA packet and repair routing before autonomous phase advancement.
goals.rationale: Advance the Luke Subconscious/auto-build roadmap without weakening trading safety or adding hidden runtime behavior.
allowed_paths: tests/ for approved QA utilities later
required_tests: focused tests for QA packet validation if code is added
cmd /c npx vitest run tests/autonomous-recommendation-only.test.js if trading posture could be affected
goals.stop_condition: QA packet records files changed, protected paths touched/not touched, tests run/skipped, behavior proven, regression risk, rollback path, and final status.
goals.rollback_expectation: Revert only this phase's owned plan/doc/code changes; do not touch unrelated work. If runtime state is written unexpectedly, stop and report before further action. If protected paths appear in git diff --name-only, stop before commit/push unless the user explicitly approved that exact surface.
non_goals: No trading execution, broker routing, risk, credential, secret, or .env changes.
No production runtime state writes unless this phase explicitly allows them.
risks: A passing report without evidence can hide regressions. Require command output summaries and skipped-test reasons.

goals.title: Runtime monitor
goals.objective: Define and later implement a monitor that detects stuck loops, repeated failures, unapproved path edits, dirty-worktree conflict, missing tests, and policy violations.
goals.rationale: Advance the Luke Subconscious/auto-build roadmap without weakening trading safety or adding hidden runtime behavior.
allowed_paths: tests/ for approved monitor utilities later
no runtime process changes without explicit approval
required_tests: focused monitor tests using fixture diffs if code is added
goals.stop_condition: Monitor can stop a run and report why, without making hidden code or runtime state changes.
goals.rollback_expectation: Revert only this phase's owned plan/doc/code changes; do not touch unrelated work. If runtime state is written unexpectedly, stop and report before further action. If protected paths appear in git diff --name-only, stop before commit/push unless the user explicitly approved that exact surface.
non_goals: No trading execution, broker routing, risk, credential, secret, or .env changes.
No production runtime state writes unless this phase explicitly allows them.
risks: A monitor that patches automatically becomes an executor. Keep monitor stop/report first.

goals.title: Scheduled auto-think
goals.objective: Add scheduled thinking only after review-only signal flow, write gates, monitor, and QA packet exist.
goals.rationale: Advance the Luke Subconscious/auto-build roadmap without weakening trading safety or adding hidden runtime behavior.
allowed_paths: scheduler-related code only after explicit approval and focused plan
required_tests: inspect scripts before any npm run
node --check scheduler.js only if scheduler is touched
focused tests for schedule gating if code is added
goals.stop_condition: Auto-think can read approved sources and produce review-only proposals without creating jobs, routing commands, writing production state without approval, or calling expensive models for empty queues.
goals.rollback_expectation: Revert only this phase's owned plan/doc/code changes; do not touch unrelated work. If runtime state is written unexpectedly, stop and report before further action. If protected paths appear in git diff --name-only, stop before commit/push unless the user explicitly approved that exact surface.
non_goals: No trading execution, broker routing, risk, credential, secret, or .env changes.
No production runtime state writes unless this phase explicitly allows them.
risks: Scheduled work can become invisible autopilot. Require monitor and digest/reporting first.

goals.title: Gated auto-build
goals.objective: Permit bounded auto-build only after all prerequisite gates exist mechanically and pass tests.
goals.rationale: Advance the Luke Subconscious/auto-build roadmap without weakening trading safety or adding hidden runtime behavior.
allowed_paths: code paths only after separate explicit implementation plan
required_tests: cmd /c npx vitest run for touched areas
goals.stop_condition: One phase runs at a time, sprint lock is active, QA must pass before the next phase, failure creates repair work, and final QA runs end-to-end.
goals.rollback_expectation: Revert only this phase's owned plan/doc/code changes; do not touch unrelated work. If runtime state is written unexpectedly, stop and report before further action. If protected paths appear in git diff --name-only, stop before commit/push unless the user explicitly approved that exact surface.
non_goals: No trading execution, broker routing, risk, credential, secret, or .env changes.
No production runtime state writes unless this phase explicitly allows them.
risks: Auto-build is unsafe until every gate exists and is tested.
assumptions: No live trading, broker, risk, Pine, NinjaTrader, credential, or runtime-state authority is implied by auto-build.
